_doc: Auto-generated by build_live_shows.py from shows.json. buckets[*] lists Broadway shows grouped by status; off_broadway is flat regardless of bucket. Do NOT edit by hand — edit shows.json and re-run the builder.

coming_soon.title: Galileo
coming_soon.subtitle: A new musical about the man who saw the stars
coming_soon.synopsis: A musical centered on the historical scientist Galileo Galilei, who defied the Inquisition with what his telescope revealed about the heavens. Premiered at Berkeley Rep in 2024.

coming_soon.title: Paddington the Musical
coming_soon.subtitle: From Darkest Peru to Broadway
coming_soon.synopsis: The London hit musical based on Michael Bond's beloved bear arrives on Broadway. A small bear from Darkest Peru finds a new family in London — until his world is upended in a story of belonging, marmalade, and the small kindnesses that make a home.

coming_soon.title: School Girls; or, the African Mean Girls Play
coming_soon.subtitle: Jocelyn Bioh's hit comedy gets a Broadway transfer
coming_soon.synopsis: Jocelyn Bioh's acclaimed comedy follows the catty rivalries at a 1986 Ghanaian boarding school where six girls compete for the Miss Ghana 1986 crown. The play originally ran Off-Broadway at MCC Theater in 2017 and won wide acclaim for its sharp humor and heart.

coming_soon.title: Wanted
coming_soon.subtitle: The Mary and Martha Clarke story (formerly Gun & Powder)
coming_soon.synopsis: A new musical inspired by the true story of Mary and Martha Clarke, African-American twin sisters in 1893 Texas who pose as white outlaws to settle their mother's sharecropper debt and save her home. Premiered at Paper Mill Playhouse in 2024 under the title Gun & Powder.

live.title: Harry Potter and the Cursed Child
live.synopsis: The eighth story in the Harry Potter saga follows a middle-aged Harry, now an overworked employee at the Ministry of Magic, and his younger son Albus who struggles with his famous family legacy. Together they embark on a time-traveling adventure that threatens to unravel the past and future.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current cast unverified — rotating leads as of April 2026

creatives.name: Jack Thorne
creatives.role: Playwright (based on story by J.K. Rowling, John Tiffany, Jack Thorne)

live.title: Hamilton
live.synopsis: Lin-Manuel Miranda's Pulitzer- and Tony-winning hip-hop musical retells the life of Founding Father Alexander Hamilton from his immigrant origins through the Revolutionary War and his fatal duel with Aaron Burr. The show blends R&B, hip-hop, jazz, and traditional show tunes with a racially diverse cast to reframe the founding of America.

live.title: The Lion King
live.synopsis: Julie Taymor's landmark production adapts Disney's animated film about young lion Simba, who flees his pride after his uncle Scar engineers the murder of his father. Featuring elaborate puppetry, masks, and Elton John and Tim Rice's songs, the show follows Simba's journey to reclaim his birthright.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current cast unverified as of April 2026; long-running production with rotating cast

marketing_firms.firm: Disney Theatrical Group (in-house marketing under Andrew Flatt)

primary_firm.firm: Disney Theatrical Group (in-house marketing under Andrew Flatt)

live.title: Moulin Rouge!
live.subtitle: The Musical
live.synopsis: Alex Timbers' opulent stage adaptation of Baz Luhrmann's 2001 film transports audiences to the decadent Moulin Rouge nightclub in 1899 Paris, where penniless poet Christian falls for courtesan Satine. The production features a mash-up of pop songs spanning multiple decades, stunning design, and acrobatic spectacle.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current leads unverified as of April 2026; show has had multiple cast changes since 2018

live.title: Every Brilliant Thing
live.synopsis: Duncan Macmillan and Jonny Donahoe's solo play follows a man who begins compiling a list of everything that makes life worth living in response to his mother's depression, and traces how that list grows and evolves through his life. Daniel Radcliffe stars in the Broadway premiere, with Mariska Hargitay scheduled to take over May 26.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Wicked
live.synopsis: Based on Gregory Maguire's novel, Wicked tells the untold story of the witches of Oz — popular Glinda and misunderstood Elphaba — before Dorothy arrives, exploring their unlikely friendship and the political forces that shape their destinies. Stephen Schwartz's score includes "Defying Gravity" and "Popular."

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current leads unverified as of April 2026; original cast has been replaced many times over

live.title: Chicago
live.synopsis: The longest-running American musical in Broadway history, Chicago is a satirical jazz-age tale of Roxie Hart and Velma Kelly, two murderesses who vie for fame and acquittal in 1920s Chicago with the help of slick lawyer Billy Flynn. Bob Fosse's choreography and Kander & Ebb's score make it one of Broadway's defining works.

creatives.name: Ann Reinking
creatives.role: Choreographer (in the style of Bob Fosse)

live.title: Death of a Salesman
live.synopsis: The seventh Broadway production of Arthur Miller's Pulitzer- and Tony-winning 1949 masterwork follows Willy Loman, an aging traveling salesman whose grip on reality loosens as he confronts the gap between his American Dream and his actual life. Director Joe Mantello leads a starry revival with Nathan Lane and Laurie Metcalf.

live.title: MJ
live.subtitle: The Musical
live.synopsis: Conceived and choreographed by Tony winner Christopher Wheeldon, MJ explores the creative genius of Michael Jackson through the making of his 1992 Dangerous World Tour rehearsals. The jukebox musical featuring 37 of Jackson's songs weaves together fiction and biography to examine artistry, controversy, and legacy.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current MJ lead unverified as of April 2026; long-running with cast rotations

live.title: Giant
live.synopsis: Mark Rosenblatt's play, which won three Olivier Awards in London, portrays the real-life antisemitism scandal surrounding beloved children's author Roald Dahl, exploring his genius and his prejudices in equal measure. John Lithgow reprises his Olivier-winning performance, directed by Nicholas Hytner.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Ragtime
live.synopsis: Lincoln Center Theater's acclaimed revival of the Tony-winning 1998 musical based on E.L. Doctorow's novel weaves together three American families — Black, immigrant, and privileged white — at the turn of the 20th century as their lives collide against a backdrop of industrialism, inequality, and shifting social order. Lear deBessonet directs a luminous cast led by Joshua Henry.

live.title: Dog Day Afternoon
live.synopsis: Stephen Adly Guirgis' new stage adaptation of the 1975 Sidney Lumet film follows two would-be bank robbers in Brooklyn whose bungled heist spirals into a hours-long hostage crisis broadcast live on TV. Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach, both from The Bear, make their Broadway debuts in this raw, darkly funny urban thriller.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: CATS: The Jellicle Ball
live.synopsis: A radical reinvention of Andrew Lloyd Webber's Cats set within New York City's LGBTQ ballroom culture, this production transforms the Jellicle Ball into a voguing competition featuring house music remixes of the iconic score and runway-ready choreography by Omari Wiles. André De Shields leads the cast as Old Deuteronomy.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Chess
live.synopsis: The first Broadway production of Benny Andersson and Björn Ulvaeus' Cold War chess musical, with a book by Richard Nelson, pits an American grandmaster against a Soviet champion in a championship match that becomes a proxy for superpower rivalry and love triangles. Aaron Tveit and Lea Michele lead the revival.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Additional cast unverified as of April 2026

producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Aladdin
live.synopsis: Disney's stage adaptation of the 1992 animated film follows street rat Aladdin as he uses a magic lamp and a wish-granting Genie to court Princess Jasmine in the fictional city of Agrabah. Featuring Alan Menken's songs expanded with new material and spectacular choreography, the production has run continuously since 2014.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current cast unverified as of April 2026; long-running with rotations

creatives.name: Howard Ashman, Tim Rice & Chad Beguelin

live.title: Stranger Things: The First Shadow
live.synopsis: A prequel to the Netflix series set in Hawkins, Indiana in 1959, the Olivier Award-winning play reveals the origins of Henry Creel — who would become Vecna — and the secretive government experiments at Hawkins Lab that would set events in motion for the next 30 years. Stephen Daldry directs the Broadway transfer from London's West End.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current Broadway cast unverified as of April 2026

creatives.name: The Duffer Brothers, Jack Thorne & Kate Trefry

live.title: The Lost Boys
live.subtitle: A New Musical
live.synopsis: Based on the 1987 Joel Schumacher supernatural horror-comedy film, this world premiere musical follows two brothers who move with their mother to Santa Carla, California only to discover their new friends are vampires. Michael Arden directs the production featuring music by indie band The Rescues.

creatives.name: The Rescues (Kyler England, AG, Gabriel Mann)

producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Proof
live.synopsis: David Auburn's Pulitzer- and Tony-winning play follows Catherine, the brilliant daughter of a renowned but mentally ill mathematician who has just died, as she faces questions about whether she authored a major mathematical proof and wrestles with her own fear of inheriting her father's illness. Directed by Thomas Kail, this revival stars Ayo Edebiri and Don Cheadle in their Broadway debuts.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Maybe Happy Ending
live.synopsis: Set in a near future Seoul where obsolete helper robots are warehoused together, this tender Korean musical follows two helper-bots who fall in love while listening to an old CD player and dreaming of fireflies. Darren Criss and Helen J Shen originated the Broadway leads.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: The Outsiders
live.synopsis: Based on S.E. Hinton's beloved 1967 novel, this new musical follows Ponyboy Curtis and his gang of working-class Greasers as they navigate poverty, violence, and coming-of-age in 1967 Tulsa in conflict with the affluent Socs. Daryl Roth and Liz Swados's staging, with choreography by Rick and Jeff Kuperman, won the Tony for Best Musical in 2024.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current cast unverified as of April 2026; show has had cast rotations since April 2024 opening

creatives.name: Zach Chance, Jonathan Clay & Justin Levine

live.title: The Great Gatsby
live.synopsis: The first stage musical adaptation of F. Scott Fitzgerald's iconic novel follows Jay Gatsby's obsessive pursuit of Daisy Buchanan in Jazz Age Long Island, wrapped in an original score with jazz and pop influences. The current cast features Reeve Carney as Gatsby and Eva Noblezada as Daisy, following Jeremy Jordan's departure in early 2025.

live.title: Hadestown
live.synopsis: Anaïs Mitchell's folk opera, winner of eight Tony Awards including Best Musical, weaves together the myths of Orpheus and Eurydice and Hades and Persephone in a Depression-era industrial underworld. Rachel Chavkin's production features a New Orleans jazz-inflected score and a rotating ensemble playing the Fates.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current cast unverified as of April 2026; long-running with cast rotations

live.title: Just in Time
live.synopsis: An immersive jukebox musical bio of Bobby Darin, transforming Circle in the Square into an intimate nightclub where the singer's short, remarkable life — from teen idol to Oscar-nominated actor — plays out through his greatest hits including 'Mack the Knife' and 'Beyond the Sea.' Jeremy Jordan took over the lead role from Jonathan Groff on April 21, 2026.

live.title: Joe Turner's Come and Gone
live.synopsis: August Wilson's 1984 play, the second in his ten-part Pittsburgh Cycle, is set in a Pittsburgh boarding house in 1911 and centers on Seth and Bertha Holly as they take in travelers navigating the upheaval of the Great Migration. When a mysterious stranger named Herald Loomis arrives searching for his lost wife, the community is forced to confront memory, identity, and spiritual healing.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Buena Vista Social Club
live.synopsis: Based on the Grammy Award-winning 1997 Cuban album and documentary, this musical follows legendary singers Omara Portuondo and Ibrahim Ferrer and their fellow musicians as they are reunited in Havana by American producer Ry Cooder to record an album that would make them world-famous. Saheem Ali directs with choreography by Justin Peck and Patricia Delgado.

live.title: Titanique
live.synopsis: The smash Off-Broadway jukebox parody reimagines the sinking of the Titanic as told by Céline Dion herself, recharting the fate of Jack and Rose through the lens of Dion's greatest hits. Marla Mindelle reprises her Off-Broadway role alongside Jim Parsons and Deborah Cox in the Broadway transfer.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: The Fear of 13
live.synopsis: Inspired by the true story of Nick Yarris, a man who spent 22 years on death row for a crime he did not commit, Lindsey Ferrentino's play dramatizes Nick's prison visits with a volunteer named Jackie and the way their deepening conversations force both of them to confront questions of justice, guilt, and human connection. Adrien Brody and Tessa Thompson make their Broadway debuts.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Death Becomes Her
live.synopsis: Based on the 1992 black comedy film, this new musical follows rival socialites Madeline Ashton and Helen Sharp who each take a magical immortality potion with increasingly grotesque consequences, all while competing for the same man. Megan Hilty and Jennifer Simard star in a show celebrated for its outrageous practical effects.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: The Book of Mormon
live.synopsis: Trey Parker, Matt Stone, and Robert Lopez's satirical smash follows two naive Mormon missionaries — one overconfident, one anxious — sent to Uganda where their earnest attempts to spread the Gospel collide with the harsh realities of poverty and civil war. The 2011 Best Musical Tony winner remains one of Broadway's top-grossing shows.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current cast unverified as of April 2026; long-running with cast rotations

creatives.name: Trey Parker, Robert Lopez & Matt Stone

live.title: The Rocky Horror Show
live.synopsis: Richard O'Brien's 1973 rock 'n' roll horror musical follows Brad and Janet, stranded at the castle of Dr. Frank-N-Furter, a mad scientist transvestite who unveils his latest creation — the muscle-bound Rocky Horror. The third Broadway production features a star-studded cast led by Luke Evans as Frank-N-Furter and Rachel Dratch as the Narrator.

live.title: Operation Mincemeat
live.subtitle: A New Musical
live.synopsis: SpitLip's Olivier-nominated farcical retelling of the true WWII British intelligence operation, in which British officers planted false documents on a dead body to deceive the Nazis about the Allied invasion of Sicily. The five-person ensemble plays multiple roles — including a rotating cast of historical figures — in this celebrated transfer from London's West End.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Original London cast reprised Broadway roles; current replacements unverified as of April 2026

creatives.name: SpitLip (Zoe Roberts, Claire Waldron, Felix Hagan, David Cumming)

producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Oh, Mary!
live.synopsis: Cole Escola's hit comedy imagines Mary Todd Lincoln as a desperately unhappy woman secretly pursuing a cabaret career behind her husband's back on the eve of the Civil War. The play has cycled through star turns by Cole Escola, Tituss Burgess, Jinkx Monsoon, Jane Krakowski, and John Cameron Mitchell; Maya Rudolph takes over April 28.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: & Juliet
live.synopsis: The irreverent jukebox musical imagines what Juliet's life would be like if she chose not to die for Romeo, following her adventures with new friends as she discovers herself and love on her own terms — set entirely to Max Martin's pop catalog including hits by Britney Spears, Katy Perry, and the Backstreet Boys.

cast.name: _note
cast.role: Current cast unverified as of April 2026; long-running with rotations

live.title: Schmigadoon!
live.synopsis: Based on the first season of the Apple TV+ series, this original stage musical follows bickering couple Josh and Melissa who stumble into Schmigadoon, a magical town where everyone lives inside a Golden Age Broadway musical — and the only way out is to find true love. The show is a loving pastiche of the classic American musical form.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: SIX
live.subtitle: The Musical
live.synopsis: Toby Marlow and Lucy Moss' pop-concert musical reimagines the six wives of Henry VIII as a modern girl band competing to tell the most tragic tale of their marriages. Running since 2021 (delayed by COVID), the show has become a cultural phenomenon celebrated for its feminism, catchy score, and rotating cast of queens.

cast.name: Adrianna Hicks
cast.role: Catherine of Aragon (current as of Feb 16, 2026)

live.title: Two Strangers (Carry a Cake Across New York)
live.synopsis: The feel-good Olivier Award-winning British musical follows Tom, an uptight Brit, and Dani, a chaotic New Yorker, who meet while carrying a cake across the city for a wedding. Their rom-com journey across Manhattan unfolds over one day, fueled by an original indie-pop score.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Beaches
live.subtitle: A New Musical
live.synopsis: Based on Iris Rainer Dart's bestselling novel and the 1988 film starring Bette Midler and Barbara Hershey, this new musical follows the decades-long friendship of scrappy singer Cee Cee Bloom and elegant Bertie White from childhood through adulthood, joy, and loss. Jessica Vosk and Kelli Barrett lead the production.
producers: _note — producers not confirmed in reviewed sources

live.title: Fallen Angels
live.synopsis: Noël Coward's 1925 comedy of manners follows two upper-class English wives — Julia and Jane — who discover they share a French ex-lover who may be on his way to visit. Confined together in a flat with too much champagne, they descend into witty, catty hysteria. Rose Byrne and Kelli O'Hara lead the Roundabout revival.

live.title: Becky Shaw
live.synopsis: Gina Gionfriddo's sharp dark comedy, making its Broadway debut, follows the disastrous aftermath of a blind date gone spectacularly wrong when well-meaning friends set up self-absorbed Becky Shaw. The play is a razor-edged examination of generosity, selfishness, and the social contracts we impose on one another.

live.title: The Balusters
live.synopsis: David Lindsay-Abaire's new play, produced by Manhattan Theatre Club, follows a neighborhood association thrown into raucous conflict over a proposal to install a stop sign on their peaceful block — a comedy that escalates from the mundane into a sharp examination of community, property, and the surprising viciousness of small-stakes disputes.

off_broadway.title: Heathers: The Musical
off_broadway.synopsis: Outcast Veronica Sawyer is taken under the wing of the three Heathers, the most powerful clique at Westerberg High — until she falls for the dangerous outsider J.D. and their dark pact begins. Based on the 1988 cult film, this revival transferred from London's West End where it won the WhatsOnStage Award for Best Musical Revival.

off_broadway.title: The Play That Goes Wrong
off_broadway.synopsis: The accident-prone Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society attempts to stage a 1920s murder mystery, but everything that can go wrong does — sets collapse, props misbehave, actors miss cues, and the audience is pulled into the chaos. The Olivier Award-winning British farce has been running off-Broadway continuously since 2019.

creatives.name: Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer, Henry Shields

off_broadway.title: Little Shop of Horrors
off_broadway.synopsis: Set on Skid Row, meek floral assistant Seymour discovers a mysterious carnivorous plant he names Audrey II after his coworker crush. As the plant grows — fed on human blood — Seymour's life spirals into darkly comic horror. Alan Menken and Howard Ashman's cult 1982 musical returns to its off-Broadway roots in a long-running revival now in its seventh year.

off_broadway.title: Primary Trust
off_broadway.subtitle: Eboni Booth's Pulitzer-winning small-town fable
off_broadway.synopsis: Eboni Booth's Pulitzer Prize-winning play about a quiet bookstore clerk in upstate New York whose comfortable, lonely routine collapses when his shop closes — forcing him to face what he's been avoiding.

off_broadway.title: Mexodus
off_broadway.synopsis: Two performers — an escaped enslaved person and a Mexican war veteran — forge an unlikely bond as they travel the lesser-known Underground Railroad branch that ran south across the Rio Grande into Mexico. Written and performed live by Brian Quijada and Nygel D. Robinson using real-time loop technology to build the show's hip-hop and Afro-Latino soundscape.

off_broadway.title: Stereophonic
off_broadway.subtitle: David Adjmi's Tony-winning rock-recording-studio drama
off_broadway.synopsis: David Adjmi's Tony Award-winning play set in 1976, following a fictional rock band as they record their breakthrough album in a Sausalito studio. Original songs by Will Butler.

off_broadway.title: The Ally
off_broadway.subtitle: Itamar Moses' campus politics powderkeg
off_broadway.synopsis: A Jewish-American writing professor is asked to sign an inflammatory open letter and finds himself caught in a campus political firestorm where every choice costs him an ally.

off_broadway.title: Appropriate
off_broadway.subtitle: Branden Jacobs-Jenkins' Southern Gothic family reckoning
off_broadway.synopsis: Branden Jacobs-Jenkins' acclaimed family drama: three estranged adult siblings reunite at the Arkansas plantation home where they grew up to settle their late father's estate — and uncover a photo album that forces them to confront what kind of family they actually came from.

off_broadway.title: An Enemy of the People
off_broadway.subtitle: Ibsen's whistleblower drama, in a new Amy Herzog adaptation
off_broadway.theatre: Theatre for a New Audience · Polonsky Shakespeare Center
off_broadway.synopsis: Amy Herzog's adaptation of Ibsen's classic about a small-town doctor who discovers the local spa is contaminated and unleashes a war between fact and fiction, science and economics, the individual and the mob.

off_broadway.title: The Lonely Few
off_broadway.subtitle: A new musical love story about second chances
off_broadway.theatre: MCC Theater · Susan and Ronald Frankel Theater
off_broadway.synopsis: A new musical about a small-town country singer trying to escape her past and an aging rocker chasing one more big break. They meet, they sing, they save each other.

off_broadway.title: All of Me
off_broadway.subtitle: A romance between two voice-augmentation users
off_broadway.theatre: New Group · Pershing Square Signature Center
off_broadway.synopsis: Laura Winters' romantic comedy about two strangers — both wheelchair users with augmented-communication devices — who fall in love against the soundtrack of their own synthesized voices.

off_broadway.title: Drunk Shakespeare
off_broadway.synopsis: Each performance, one cast member from the rotating company of professional actors downs five shots of whiskey before attempting to perform a scene from a Shakespeare play, while their sober castmates keep the show from completely unraveling. Adult-only (21+) and staged speakeasy-style with a full craft cocktail bar.

off_broadway.title: Spamalot
off_broadway.subtitle: Eric Idle's Monty Python musical, off-Broadway revival
off_broadway.synopsis: Eric Idle's Monty Python and the Holy Grail-inspired musical comedy in an intimate off-Broadway revival. Killer rabbits, knights who say Ni, and the always look on the bright side of life.

off_broadway.title: Perfect Crime
off_broadway.synopsis: Psychiatrist Margaret Thorne Brent may — or may not — have murdered her wealthy husband. A detective's investigation into her sleek Connecticut home unravels into layers of psychological gamesmanship and possible conspiracy. The longest-running play in New York history, continuously performed since 1987 with the same lead actress.

off_broadway.title: Hamlet
off_broadway.subtitle: National Theatre of Great Britain
off_broadway.synopsis: The National Theatre of Great Britain's production of Shakespeare's tragedy of indecision, grief, and revenge. Director Robert Hastie leads the visiting UK company in a limited engagement at BAM's landmark Harvey Theater.

off_broadway.title: Indian Princesses
off_broadway.synopsis: A new play by Eliana Theologides Rodriguez, co-produced with Rattlestick Theater, that examines identity, cultural inheritance, and contested histories through an ensemble whose lives intersect in unexpected ways.

off_broadway.title: KENREX
off_broadway.synopsis: On July 10, 1981, in Skidmore, Missouri, a man was shot dead in broad daylight before 60 witnesses — all of whom later claimed to have seen nothing. Olivier Award winner Jack Holden plays over 30 characters in this one-man true-crime thriller, backed by a live Americana score performed on stage by composer John Patrick Elliott.

off_broadway.title: Lights Out: Nat 'King' Cole
off_broadway.synopsis: Set on the final night of Nat 'King' Cole's NBC variety show — the first network TV program hosted by a Black American — Cole must decide whether to go quietly or follow Sammy Davis Jr.'s advice and go out with a bang. The play weaves Cole's hit songs into a story about art, race, and the cost of breaking through.

off_broadway.title: New Born
off_broadway.synopsis: Three interlocking monologues — a tree surgeon falling in love, a young woman searching the prairie for a lost friend, and a new mother consumed by a celebrity obsession — whose smallest choices cascade across centuries and continents. Ella Hickson's play, presented at Audible's Minetta Lane in repertory.

off_broadway.title: The Receptionist
off_broadway.synopsis: Adam Bock's poisoned comedy about the seemingly mundane rhythms of an office reception desk that gradually reveals the moral compromises humming beneath the surface of polite workplace life.
